design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate side-scrolling levels, defeat enemies and hazards, collect power-ups and optional items, reach and defeat area bosses to progress through skeleton-shaped island archipelago
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (health/lives) balanced against combat and traversal hazards; optional item collection adds exploration tension
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as a headless mummy protagonist with unique face-extension attack mechanic, reforming a shattered island and defeating an underworld demon army
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Distinctive visual identity (gothic/mummy aesthetic as westernized reskin); unique protagonist design with torso face and boomerang skull mechanic; skeleton island geography as thematic progression organizer; offers player choice in difficulty via heart settings

difficulty.lives_system: Small number of lives per playthrough; losing all health units depletes life counter
difficulty.checkpointing: Area-based progression with bosses at end of level clusters; levels named after skeleton anatomy (Abdomainland, Armington, etc.)
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Significant: Players can select 1-3 hearts per life before game start at options menu; additional health upgrades found in levels allow maximum 5 hearts (10 total hits per life). This is explicit divergence from Magical Hat predecessor which had one-hit kills.

progression.upgrade_system: Health upgrades (hearts) found in certain levels; collectible potions (invincibility, screen-clear effects) usable at player discretion; boomerang skull power-up that persists until damage taken
progression.unlock_mechanism: Some levels require collection of specific items for Dr. Frank N. Stein before progression; boss defeats unlock next area
replayability_hooks: Speedrun community active (22 runs documented, any% category, fastest ~22 minutes)
Multiple difficulty presets via heart selection system

world_building_techniques: Skeleton-shaped island geography reflected in level naming (anatomical references: Abdomainland, Armington)
Character design storytelling (headless mummy protagonist, mad scientist creator, underworld demon antagonist)
Comic strip adaptation (Sonic the Comic, 1993-1998) expanded lore with comedic absurdist tone

boss_design.philosophy: Area culmination encounters; Max D. Cap serves as final boss after island reformation; bosses appear at end of level clusters
notable_bosses: Max D. Cap (Underworld demon, final antagonist commanding monster army)

faction_indicators: Surface world (player Chuck D. Head, Dr. Frank N. Stein, Igor)
Underworld faction (Max D. Cap, monster army)
danger_signals: Island fragmentation caused by underworld invasion
Health/life depletion mechanics signal immediate danger
environmental_storytelling: Skeleton island geography as visual metaphor for corruption/fragmentation
Anatomically-named zones reinforce body-horror gothic theme
Castle Frank N. Stein setting (evident from comic adaptation lore)

sources_used: Wikipedia - Decap Attack article (plot, gameplay mechanics, health system, differences from Magical Hat, boss information, character descriptions, level design, power-up systems)
